description:
A World Cup 2026 app built with React 19 and TypeScript — live scores pulled from a real results feed, score predictions graded automatically, and a built-in AI assistant that answers from live data. It also ships as an installable Progressive Web App so you can add it straight to your phone.

The app covers everything you need to follow the tournament: date-sliding between match days, one-tap score syncing, favorite-team highlighting with a gold-star border, live group standings showing who's advancing, and a prediction tracker that grades your calls after every match.

🎯 What you'll learn
- Syncing live match data with a visibility-aware auto-refresh effect
- Building a score prediction system with automatic accuracy grading
- Highlighting a favorite team across all match views with Zustand
- Rendering live group standings with advancement highlighting
- Wiring up an AI assistant grounded in a sliding window of live fixtures
- Deploying an Express API and a React PWA to Render


The World Cup 2026 app is built on React 19 with TypeScript, using Vite for the build, Tailwind CSS for styling, and a Zustand store with persistence for predictions and favorites. A lightweight Express backend serves the live scores PWA data and proxies the AI requests — deployed on Render and installable as a Progressive Web App. If you want to follow the tournament with your own live scores PWA, this is the full build.

▶️ Full breakdown: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xC3stqkpJrg
